---
title: "Week 6: Web Interface Design & User Portals"
subtitle: "Building the User Experience (UX) Layer with Softr"
author: "Master's in No-Code Programming"
date: "2026-04-22"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    theme: flatly
    code-fold: false
---

# 🎯 Learning Objectives
By the end of this session, students will be able to:
1.  Apply **Atomic Design** principles to no-code web interfaces.
2.  Connect a **Frontend (Softr)** to a **Backend (Airtable/Xano)** in real-time.
3.  Implement **Conditional Visibility** based on user roles and permissions.
4.  Design a seamless **Login/Signup flow** with protected pages.

---

# 1. Lecture: The Psychology of the Interface (1 Hour)

## 1.1 Atomic Design in No-Code
We don't just "draw" pages; we build systems. We follow the **Atomic Design** methodology:
* **Atoms:** Buttons, inputs, labels.
* **Molecules:** A search bar (Input + Button).
* **Organisms:** A navigation bar or a "List of Items" block.
* **Templates/Pages:** The final assembly of these components.



## 1.2 User Roles & Permissions
In a Master's level app, not everyone sees everything.
* **Logged-out Users:** Can see the Landing Page and "About" section.
* **Students:** Can see "Available Items" and "My Profile."
* **Admins:** Can see "Pending Approvals" and "User Management."
* **Logic:** We define visibility at the **Block level** and **Page level**.

## 1.3 UX Best Practices for Portals
* **Consistency:** Using a "Global Style" guide (colors, typography).
* **Feedback Loops:** Showing "Success" messages after a form is submitted.
* **Responsive Design:** Ensuring the portal works on a laptop and a tablet.



---

# 2. Lab: Building the Secure Portal (2 Hours)

## 2.1 Connecting the "Face" to the "Brain" (30 mins)
* **Activity:** Link Softr to your Week 3 Airtable base.
* **Task:** Set up the API connection and map your "Users" table to the Softr User Management system.

## 2.2 Building the "Browse & Detail" Flow (1 Hour)
* **Step 1:** Add a **Dynamic List Block**. Map the fields (Title, Image, Price) to your Airtable columns.
* **Step 2:** Configure the **Detail Page**. When a user clicks an item, Softr must open a dedicated page showing more info.
* **Step 3:** Add a **Search & Filter** bar to allow users to find items by category.

## 2.3 Implementing Permissions (30 mins)
* **Activity:** Creating a "Seller's Dashboard."
* **Task:** Add a block that is **only visible** to users whose "User Role" is "Seller."
* **Logic:** Test the app as two different users to ensure the visibility rules are functioning correctly.

---

# 3. Independent Study: Navigation & UI Mapping

::: {.callout-important}
### Assignment Due: Week 6
Finalize the **UI Prototype** of your application.
:::

**Submission Requirements:**
1.  **Sitemap:** A diagram showing how pages link together (Home -> Login -> Dashboard -> Item Detail).
2.  **Live URL:** A working Softr link where a user can sign up and see a list of data.
3.  **UI Style Guide:** A brief document listing your chosen Primary Color, Secondary Color, and Fonts.

---

# 💡 Teacher's Tip
*Students often get distracted by "making it pretty." Remind them: **"Function over Form."** A beautiful app that doesn't display data is a failure. An ugly app that correctly filters data by User ID is a technical success. Focus on the 'Data Mapping' first, and the 'CSS' last.*

---

**Next Week:** *Native Mobile Development with FlutterFlow. We learn how to take our logic into the pocket of the user.*
